content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 24, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
May 24 saw significant expansion of the ElizaOS ecosystem, headlined by the integration of the NEAR AI inference provider and the addition of ERC-8004 identity registry support. Development focused on hardening the agent planner loop, improving cloud infrastructure reliability, and refining the OS release pipeline with enhanced SLSA provenance and SBOM verification.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**AI Inference and Cloud Infrastructure**
*   Added the NEAR AI inference provider plugin ([#7883](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7883)).
*   Implemented ERC-8004 identity registry endpoints and a TypeScript client for Eliza Cloud ([#7937](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7937)).
*   Replaced HTTP control-plane health probes with Redis heartbeats to resolve production failures ([#7899](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7899)).
*   Resolved wallet proxy issues by fixing steward agent ID resolution and adding fallback logic ([#7933](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7933), [#7934](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7934)).

**Core Runtime and Planner Improvements**
*   Bound runaway tool-argument validation loops and restored evaluator message precedence ([#7897](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7897), [#7924](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7924)).
*   Refactored response-handler evaluators to use prompt-rule contracts, improving context preservation and preventing phantom action claims ([#7923](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7923), [#7931](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7931)).

**OS and Supply Chain Security**
*   Enhanced release integrity by minting SLSA build provenance for ISO, VM, and Debian artifacts ([#7906](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7906), [#7907](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7907), [#7908](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7908)).
*   Restored the `milady-tails` variant and added comprehensive release verification documentation ([#7909](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7909), [#7936](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7936)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   **Planner & Prompting:** [#7945](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7945), [#7944](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7944), [#7942](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7942).
*   **Orchestration & Cloud:** [#7943](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7943), [#7940](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7940).
*   **OS & Tooling:** [#7932](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7932), [#7927](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7927), [#7921](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7921).

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Safety and Discussion**
*   [#7922](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7922): Discussion regarding safety scaffolds for personal-crisis topics; closed due to incorrect GitHub identity.

## NEW ISSUES

**Community Plugins**
*   [#7938](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7938): Proposal for `plugin-prflght` (Solana transaction firewall).
*   [#7939](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7939): Proposal for `plugin-gblin` (Base treasury management).

**Planner Quality**
*   [#7935](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7935): Issues with chat-recall requests defaulting to SHELL and leaking sub-agent metadata.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**[#7925](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7925) Discussion: Default safety scaffold for high-stakes personal-crisis topics**
*   The discussion concluded that a framework-level default safety scaffold conflicts with the "edgy-agent" use case. 
*   Maintainers determined that per-agent character configuration is the appropriate layer for safety overrides. 
*   The specific bug regarding BROWSER tool spawning was addressed structurally through planner-routing improvements in PRs [#7942](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7942) and [#7944](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7944).

**[#7935](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7935) Planner quality: explicit chat-recall requests**
*   Progress has been made on two fronts: the planner now correctly avoids SHELL fallback for chat-recall (reducing costs and trajectory size), and sub-agent metadata leaks are being addressed via PR [#7943](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7943). 
*   The remaining challenge is preventing sub-agents from emitting routing-kind constants as markdown headers in user-facing text.
